title: The Philippines.
body:
O.P.
Hey there. Noticing that the Philippines appears to open, with the only requirement being a supervised RAT test before departure, for vaccinated travelers. I was thinking about Thailand, but as it stands, due to only having two weeks, I consider Thailand too big a risk. For example, mandatory quarantine in a hotel, whilst one awaits for a result of a PCR test. Then a RAT test on day five..note I have had COVID and am concerned that with the PCR test Thailand uses it may show positive. With quarantine at your expense if you test positive. Rumour has it that this may all change on May the 1st. With this in mind, the Phills has come top on mind. Havent been there in many years, however it was a great stopoff back in the day, I got to explore Manila and its environs. Anyone with recent experience travelling there? I was thinking Boracay, Palawan, a chilled holiday. If you have, how did you find travel within the Phils? I have heard of paperwork before going to Boracay etc...from Manila. Or do you have recommendations on what to see, visit.
